div.head: Memorandum by the Assistant Secretary of State (Berle)
div.p: I gather that the British Government agrees entirely with the line taken by the Thai Minister; he is particularly anxious to let drive at the Prime Minister of Thailand, who seems to have sold out the country.

Under the circumstances, unless FEDivision of Far Eastern Affairs. has some reason to the contrary, I propose that we continue to recognize the Thai Minister; that we let him go ahead and develop such Free Thai movement as he can; and that we use the Department’s power to certify his right to dispose of a limited amount of money. I should be glad to have a recommendation as to the amount of money we ought to advise the Thai Minister that he might need.

Unless FE disagrees, I should be glad if FDFinancial Division. would go ahead and get the material together for the appropriate certifications. The assistance of Treasury will be needed in locating the assets.
